title: Implement CircuitBreakerManager singleton for per-host circuit breaker management
description: Create the CircuitBreakerManager singleton class that manages one CircuitBreaker instance per host. This prevents creating duplicate circuit breakers when multiple connections target the same host. The manager uses a ConcurrentDictionary<string, CircuitBreaker> internally and provides a GetCircuitBreaker(host) method that returns an existing or newly created CircuitBreaker. Configuration for the circuit breaker (failure threshold, timeout) comes from TelemetryConfiguration. Write comprehensive unit tests verifying: same host returns same breaker, different hosts get separate breakers, thread-safe concurrent access.
design_context: Section 3.3 of the design doc: CircuitBreakerManager is a singleton that manages circuit breakers per host. Uses ConcurrentDictionary<string, CircuitBreaker> for per-host breakers. Sprint plan Task 2: CircuitBreakerManager.
input_requirements: CircuitBreaker class exists at src/Telemetry/CircuitBreaker.cs (already complete)
TelemetryConfiguration class exists at src/Telemetry/TelemetryConfiguration.cs (already complete)
exit_criteria: CircuitBreakerManager is a sealed internal class with singleton pattern (private static instance, public GetInstance() method)
GetCircuitBreaker(string host) returns existing CircuitBreaker for known host or creates new one
Same host always returns the same CircuitBreaker instance
Different hosts get separate CircuitBreaker instances
Thread-safe for concurrent access from multiple connections
All unit tests pass
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'
test_requirements: GetCircuitBreaker_NewHost_CreatesBreaker: new host -> new CircuitBreaker instance
GetCircuitBreaker_SameHost_ReturnsSameBreaker: same host twice -> same instance
GetCircuitBreaker_DifferentHosts_CreatesSeparateBreakers: 'host1' and 'host2' -> different instances
GetCircuitBreaker_ThreadSafe_NoDuplicates: 10 concurrent calls for same host -> single instance
GetInstance_ReturnsSingleton: multiple calls return same manager instance

title: Implement CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter wrapper for ITelemetryExporter
description: Create CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter that implements ITelemetryExporter and wraps an inner exporter with circuit breaker protection. When the circuit is closed, it delegates to the inner exporter. When the circuit is open, it drops events gracefully (returns false, no exception). The circuit breaker must see exceptions BEFORE they are swallowed, so it can track failures properly. The wrapper gets its CircuitBreaker from CircuitBreakerManager.GetInstance().GetCircuitBreaker(host). Write comprehensive unit tests using mock ITelemetryExporter.
design_context: Section 3.3 and 8.1 of the design doc: CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter wraps ITelemetryExporter. When circuit closed, delegates to inner exporter. When open, drops events. Critical: circuit breaker MUST see exceptions before they are swallowed. Sprint plan Task 3.
input_requirements: ITelemetryExporter interface at src/Telemetry/ITelemetryExporter.cs with ExportAsync returning Task<bool>
exit_criteria: CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter implements ITelemetryExporter
Constructor takes (string host, ITelemetryExporter innerExporter)
Gets CircuitBreaker from CircuitBreakerManager.GetInstance().GetCircuitBreaker(host)
Delegates to inner exporter when circuit is closed
Returns false (no exception) when circuit is open - events dropped gracefully
Circuit breaker sees exceptions from inner exporter (tracks failures) before they are swallowed
No exceptions propagated to caller
Logs dropped events at DEBUG level using Debug.WriteLine
All unit tests pass
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'
test_requirements: CircuitClosed_ExportsMetrics: circuit closed -> inner exporter called, returns true
CircuitOpen_DropsMetrics: force circuit open -> no export, returns false, no exception
InnerExporterFails_CircuitBreakerTracksFailure: inner exporter throws -> circuit breaker failure tracked
InnerExporterFails_NoExceptionPropagated: inner exporter throws -> caller gets false, no exception
NullOrEmptyLogs_ReturnsTrue: null or empty logs -> returns true (delegates to inner)
CancellationToken_Propagated: cancellation token passed to inner exporter

title: Define ITelemetryClient interface for batched telemetry export
description: Create the ITelemetryClient interface that defines the contract for batched telemetry event export. The interface extends IAsyncDisposable and provides three methods: Enqueue(TelemetryFrontendLog log) for non-blocking thread-safe event queuing, FlushAsync() for forcing immediate export of pending events, and CloseAsync() for graceful shutdown with a final flush. This interface is used by MetricsAggregator to send completed proto messages to the shared per-host client.
design_context: Section 3.2 of the design doc: ITelemetryClient is the per-host shared client interface. Receives events from multiple MetricsAggregators via Enqueue. Batches events and exports periodically. Sprint plan Task 4 (interface part).
exit_criteria: ITelemetryClient interface is defined in AdbcDrivers.Databricks.Telemetry namespace
Interface extends IAsyncDisposable
void Enqueue(TelemetryFrontendLog log) method declared
Task FlushAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) method declared
Task CloseAsync() method declared
XML documentation comments on interface and all methods
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'

title: Implement TelemetryClient with batching queue and periodic flush
description: Create the TelemetryClient class implementing ITelemetryClient. It uses a ConcurrentQueue<TelemetryFrontendLog> for batching events from multiple connections. Key behaviors: (1) Enqueue adds to queue non-blocking and triggers flush when batch size reached, (2) periodic flush timer (default 5s from TelemetryConfiguration.FlushIntervalMs), (3) FlushAsync drains queue up to batch size and calls ITelemetryExporter.ExportAsync, (4) uses SemaphoreSlim to prevent concurrent flushes, (5) CloseAsync stops timer, cancels pending operations, does final flush, (6) all exceptions swallowed internally with Debug.WriteLine at TRACE level. Write comprehensive unit tests with mock ITelemetryExporter.
design_context: Section 3.2 of design doc: TelemetryClient batches events from all connections to same host. Uses ConcurrentQueue, Timer for periodic flush, SemaphoreSlim for flush lock. Sprint plan Task 4.
exit_criteria: TelemetryClient implements ITelemetryClient
Constructor takes (ITelemetryExporter exporter, TelemetryConfiguration config)
ConcurrentQueue<TelemetryFrontendLog> for thread-safe queuing
Timer-based periodic flush at config.FlushIntervalMs interval
Enqueue triggers flush when queue.Count >= config.BatchSize
FlushAsync uses SemaphoreSlim(1,1) to prevent concurrent flushes
FlushAsync drains up to config.BatchSize items and calls exporter.ExportAsync
CloseAsync: dispose timer, cancel CancellationTokenSource, final flush, dispose resources
DisposeAsync delegates to CloseAsync
All exceptions swallowed with Debug.WriteLine trace logging
_disposed volatile flag prevents operations after disposal
All unit tests pass
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'
test_requirements: Enqueue_AddsToQueue_NonBlocking: enqueue returns immediately
Enqueue_BatchSizeReached_TriggersFlush: enqueue config.BatchSize events -> ExportAsync called
FlushAsync_DrainsQueueAndExports: flush with items in queue -> exporter called with batch
FlushAsync_EmptyQueue_NoExport: flush with nothing -> exporter not called
FlushAsync_ConcurrentCalls_OnlyOneFlushAtATime: concurrent flush -> SemaphoreSlim prevents double flush
CloseAsync_FlushesRemainingEvents: events in queue then close -> all events exported
CloseAsync_DisposesResources: close -> timer and semaphore disposed
Exception_Swallowed_NoThrow: exporter throws -> no exception propagated from Enqueue or FlushAsync
AfterDispose_EnqueueIsNoOp: enqueue after close -> silently ignored
FlushTimer_Elapses_ExportsEvents: wait for timer -> ExportAsync called

title: Implement TelemetryClientManager singleton with per-host client sharing and reference counting
description: Create TelemetryClientManager as a singleton that manages one ITelemetryClient per host. Uses ConcurrentDictionary<string, TelemetryClientHolder> where TelemetryClientHolder holds the client and a reference count. GetOrCreateClient(host, exporterFactory, config) returns existing client (incrementing refcount) or creates new TelemetryClient via the factory. ReleaseClientAsync(host) decrements refcount and calls CloseAsync when it reaches zero. Also create TelemetryClientHolder class. Add test support: internal UseTestInstance() method for singleton replacement in tests, and Reset() method for clearing all clients. Write comprehensive unit tests.
design_context: Section 3.2 of design doc: TelemetryClientManager is global singleton managing per-host clients with reference counting. TelemetryClientHolder holds client + refCount. GetOrCreateClient uses AddOrUpdate with Interlocked.Increment. ReleaseClientAsync uses Interlocked.Decrement, removes and closes when 0. Sprint plan Task 1.
exit_criteria: TelemetryClientManager is sealed internal with singleton pattern (private static field, public static GetInstance())
ConcurrentDictionary<string, TelemetryClientHolder> for per-host clients
GetOrCreateClient(string host, Func<ITelemetryExporter> exporterFactory, TelemetryConfiguration config) creates or returns existing client
New host: creates TelemetryClient(exporterFactory(), config) wrapped in TelemetryClientHolder with refCount=1
Existing host: increments refCount via Interlocked.Increment, returns same client
ReleaseClientAsync(string host): decrements refCount, removes and calls CloseAsync when refCount reaches 0
TelemetryClientHolder is sealed internal with internal int _refCount=1 and ITelemetryClient Client property
Thread-safe for concurrent GetOrCreateClient and ReleaseClientAsync
Internal UseTestInstance(TelemetryClientManager) returns IDisposable for test injection
Internal Reset() method clears all clients
All unit tests pass
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'
test_requirements: GetOrCreateClient_NewHost_CreatesClient: 'host1' -> new client with refCount=1
GetOrCreateClient_ExistingHost_ReturnsSameClient: same host twice -> same instance, refCount=2
ReleaseClientAsync_LastReference_ClosesClient: single ref then release -> CloseAsync called, removed from cache
ReleaseClientAsync_MultipleReferences_KeepsClient: two refs, release one -> refCount=1, client still active
GetOrCreateClient_ThreadSafe_NoDuplicates: 10 concurrent calls -> single client instance
ReleaseClientAsync_UnknownHost_NoError: release unknown host -> no exception
GetInstance_ReturnsSingleton: multiple calls -> same instance
Reset_ClearsAllClients: add clients then reset -> all removed and closed

title: Implement StatementTelemetryContext for multi-activity data aggregation
description: Create StatementTelemetryContext class that holds all aggregated telemetry data for a single statement execution. This is the core data model that merges data from MULTIPLE activities (root + children) into a single complete proto message. Fields include: identifiers (StatementId, SessionId, AuthType), system configuration (DriverSystemConfiguration), connection parameters (DriverConnectionParameters), statement execution metrics (StatementType, OperationType, ResultFormat, CompressionEnabled, TotalLatencyMs, RetryCount), result latency (ResultReadyLatencyMs, ResultConsumptionLatencyMs), chunk details for CloudFetch (TotalChunksPresent, TotalChunksIterated, SumChunksDownloadTimeMs, InitialChunkLatencyMs, SlowestChunkLatencyMs), polling metrics (PollCount, PollLatencyMs), and error info (HasError, ErrorName, ErrorMessage). Implement MergeFrom(Activity) method that merges based on activity.OperationName. Implement BuildTelemetryLog() method that creates OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog proto. Write unit tests for merging and building.
design_context: Section 3.5 and 4.4.12 of the design doc: StatementTelemetryContext holds aggregated data for single statement. MergeFrom(Activity) merges based on OperationName. BuildProto creates OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog. Fields for identifiers, system config, connection params, statement execution, result latency, chunk details, polling, and errors. Sections 4.4.2-4.4.11 provide complete field mappings from Activity tags to proto fields.
input_requirements: Proto-generated classes in src/Telemetry/Proto/ (OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog, DriverSystemConfiguration, DriverConnectionParameters, SqlExecutionEvent, ChunkDetails, ResultLatency, OperationDetail, DriverErrorInfo)
exit_criteria: StatementTelemetryContext is sealed internal class
All fields from design doc section 4.4.12 present with nullable types where appropriate
MergeFrom(Activity activity) method that routes by activity.OperationName
MergeFrom handles: ExecuteQuery/ExecuteUpdate (statement metrics), DownloadFiles (chunk details from tags and events), PollOperationStatus (polling metrics), and error status
Always captures SessionId and StatementId from any activity
Handles cloudfetch.download_summary ActivityEvent for chunk details
BuildTelemetryLog() creates complete OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog proto message

BuildTelemetryLog populates: session_id, sql_statement_id, auth_type, operation_latency_ms, system_configuration, driver_connection_params, sql_operation (with ChunkDetails, ResultLatency, OperationDetail), error_info
Includes static helper methods: ParseExecutionResult, ParseDriverMode, ParseAuthMech, ParseAuthFlow, ParseStatementType, ParseOperationType
TruncateMessage utility for error messages (200 char max)
All unit tests pass
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'
test_requirements: MergeFrom_ExecuteQuery_CapturesLatencyAndStatementType: root activity with Duration -> OperationLatencyMs set
MergeFrom_ExecuteQuery_CapturesSessionAndStatementId: activity with session.id and statement.id tags -> both captured
MergeFrom_DownloadFiles_CapturesChunkDetailsFromTags: activity with cloudfetch.total_chunks tag -> TotalChunksPresent set
MergeFrom_DownloadFiles_CapturesChunkDetailsFromEvents: activity with cloudfetch.download_summary event -> chunk metrics extracted
MergeFrom_PollOperationStatus_CapturesPollMetrics: activity with poll.count and poll.latency_ms -> PollCount and PollLatencyMs set
MergeFrom_ErrorActivity_CapturesErrorInfo: activity with Error status -> HasError=true, ErrorName and ErrorMessage set
MergeFrom_MultipleActivities_AggregatesCorrectly: merge root + child activities -> all fields populated
BuildTelemetryLog_CreatesCompleteProto: fully populated context -> OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog with all fields
BuildTelemetryLog_WithError_IncludesDriverErrorInfo: error context -> proto has error_info
BuildTelemetryLog_WithChunkDetails_IncludesCloudFetchMetrics: chunk details -> proto has chunk_details in sql_operation
ParseExecutionResult_MapsCorrectly: 'cloudfetch'->ExternalLinks, 'inline_arrow'->InlineArrow
TruncateMessage_LongMessage_Truncated: 300 char message -> 200 chars

title: Implement MetricsAggregator for per-connection activity aggregation and proto emission
description: Create MetricsAggregator class that aggregates Activity data by statement_id, builds proto messages, and enqueues them to the shared ITelemetryClient. Uses ConcurrentDictionary<string, StatementTelemetryContext> keyed by statement_id. ProcessActivity(Activity) extracts statement_id tag, gets/creates StatementTelemetryContext, calls MergeFrom, and detects root activity completion to emit proto. Root activity detection: parent == null OR parent.Source.Name != 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver'. On root completion, calls context.BuildTelemetryLog(), wraps in TelemetryFrontendLog, and enqueues to ITelemetryClient. Stores session-level configuration (sessionId, systemConfig, connectionParams) that gets injected into each StatementTelemetryContext. FlushAsync emits all pending contexts (called on connection close). All exceptions swallowed. Write comprehensive unit tests.
design_context: Section 3.5 of design doc: MetricsAggregator is per-connection. ConcurrentDictionary<string, StatementTelemetryContext> keyed by statement_id. ProcessActivity for every activity. IsRootActivity check. Emit proto on root completion. FlushAsync for connection close. Section 4.4.11 shows complete proto building. Sprint plan Task 5.
input_requirements: TelemetryFrontendLog and related models at src/Telemetry/Models/
exit_criteria: MetricsAggregator is sealed internal class implementing IDisposable
Constructor takes (ITelemetryClient telemetryClient, TelemetryConfiguration config)
SetSessionContext(string sessionId, DriverSystemConfiguration systemConfig, DriverConnectionParameters connectionParams) method for session-level data
ProcessActivity(Activity activity) method: extracts statement.id, gets/creates context, merges, checks root completion
IsRootActivity: parent == null || parent.Source.Name != 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver'
IsActivityComplete: status == Ok || status == Error
On root completion: builds TelemetryFrontendLog, enqueues to ITelemetryClient, removes context
TelemetryFrontendLog populated with: WorkspaceId, FrontendLogEventId (Guid), Context (client context + timestamp), Entry (containing OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog)
FlushAsync() emits all remaining pending contexts and clears dictionary
All exceptions swallowed with Debug.WriteLine at TRACE level
Activities without statement.id tag are silently ignored
All unit tests pass
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'

ProcessActivity_WithoutStatementId_Ignored: activity without statement.id -> no context created
ProcessActivity_SameStatementId_MergesIntoSameContext: multiple activities with same statement.id -> single context
ProcessActivity_RootActivityComplete_EmitsProtoAndRemovesContext: root activity stops -> TelemetryClient.Enqueue called, context removed
ProcessActivity_ChildActivity_DoesNotEmit: child activity stops -> no Enqueue call
ProcessActivity_RootWithError_EmitsWithErrorInfo: root with Error status -> proto includes error_info
FlushAsync_EmitsPendingContexts: contexts in dictionary -> all emitted via Enqueue
FlushAsync_ClearsDictionary: after flush -> dictionary empty
ProcessActivity_ExceptionSwallowed: aggregator error -> no exception propagated
SetSessionContext_PropagatedToNewContexts: set session config -> new contexts inherit session data

title: Implement DatabricksActivityListener as global singleton routing activities to per-connection aggregators
description: Create DatabricksActivityListener as a global singleton that subscribes to the 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver' ActivitySource and routes activities to the appropriate MetricsAggregator based on session.id tag. The listener uses a ConcurrentDictionary<string, MetricsAggregator> registry. Connections register their aggregator via RegisterAggregator(sessionId, aggregator) on open, and unregister via UnregisterAggregatorAsync(sessionId) on close (which flushes remaining metrics). The ActivityListener is configured with: ShouldListenTo filtering for 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver', ActivityStopped callback that routes to correct aggregator, and Sample callback returning AllDataAndRecorded. Start() creates and registers the ActivityListener. All exceptions swallowed in callbacks.
design_context: Section 3.4 of design doc: DatabricksActivityListener is global singleton. Routes activities by session.id to registered MetricsAggregators. ConcurrentDictionary<string, MetricsAggregator> for registry. Start() creates ActivityListener. OnActivityStopped extracts session.id and delegates to correct aggregator. Sprint plan Task 6.
exit_criteria: DatabricksActivityListener is sealed internal class implementing IDisposable
Lazy<DatabricksActivityListener> singleton pattern with static Instance property
ConcurrentDictionary<string, MetricsAggregator> _aggregators for registry
RegisterAggregator(string sessionId, MetricsAggregator aggregator) adds to registry
UnregisterAggregatorAsync(string sessionId) removes from registry and calls FlushAsync on aggregator
Start() creates ActivityListener: ShouldListenTo = source.Name == 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver', ActivityStopped = OnActivityStopped, Sample = AllDataAndRecorded
OnActivityStopped extracts session.id tag, routes to correct aggregator via ProcessActivity
Activities without session.id are silently ignored
All exceptions in callbacks swallowed with Debug.WriteLine at TRACE level
Dispose() disposes the ActivityListener
All unit tests pass
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'
test_requirements: Start_ListensToDatabricksActivitySource: ShouldListenTo returns true for 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver'
Start_IgnoresOtherActivitySources: ShouldListenTo returns false for other sources

UnregisterAggregatorAsync_RemovesAndFlushes: unregister -> removed from registry, FlushAsync called on aggregator

ActivityStopped_NoSessionId_Ignored: activity without session.id -> no aggregator called
ActivityStopped_UnknownSessionId_Ignored: activity with unregistered session.id -> no error
ActivityStopped_ExceptionSwallowed: aggregator throws -> no exception propagated
Sample_ReturnsAllDataAndRecorded: sample callback -> ActivitySamplingResult.AllDataAndRecorded
Dispose_DisposesListener: dispose -> ActivityListener disposed

title: Integrate telemetry pipeline into DatabricksConnection lifecycle
description: Modify DatabricksConnection.cs to wire up the complete telemetry pipeline. In OpenAsync: (1) Parse TelemetryConfiguration from connection properties, (2) check if telemetry enabled via config and feature flag, (3) get shared ITelemetryClient from TelemetryClientManager.GetInstance().GetOrCreateClient(host, exporterFactory, config) where exporterFactory creates CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter wrapping DatabricksTelemetryExporter, (4) create per-connection MetricsAggregator(telemetryClient, config), (5) call aggregator.SetSessionContext() with session data, (6) register aggregator with DatabricksActivityListener.Instance.RegisterAggregator(sessionId, aggregator), (7) start listener if not already started. In Dispose: (1) unregister aggregator from listener, (2) flush aggregator, (3) release telemetry client from TelemetryClientManager, (4) release feature flag context. Add internal TestExporterFactory property for test injection. All telemetry exceptions swallowed.
design_context: Section 3.2 (Usage in DatabricksConnection), Section 6.2, Section 9.2 of design doc: Connection creates per-connection MetricsAggregator and registers with global DatabricksActivityListener. Gets shared ITelemetryClient from TelemetryClientManager. CreateTelemetryExporter uses TestExporterFactory or creates real exporter. Sprint plan Task 7.
exit_criteria: DatabricksConnection has private fields: ITelemetryClient? _telemetryClient, MetricsAggregator? _metricsAggregator, TelemetryConfiguration? _telemetryConfig
Internal Func<ITelemetryExporter>? TestExporterFactory property for test injection

In OpenAsync (after base.OpenAsyncCore): parse TelemetryConfiguration, check enabled + feature flag, create exporter (via TestExporterFactory or real), get shared client from TelemetryClientManager, create MetricsAggregator, set session context, register with DatabricksActivityListener
CreateTelemetryExporter() method: uses TestExporterFactory if set, else creates CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter wrapping DatabricksTelemetryExporter
In DisposeAsyncCore: unregister from listener, flush aggregator, release client from TelemetryClientManager, release feature flag context
All telemetry code wrapped in try-catch with Debug.WriteLine at TRACE level
Telemetry initialization failure does not prevent connection from opening
Project builds successfully with 'dotnet build'
test_requirements: Integration is correct based on manual code review - detailed E2E tests in task-5.1

title: Enhance Activity tags for telemetry data collection in Connection, Statement, and CloudFetch
description: Add telemetry-specific tags to existing driver activities so the MetricsAggregator can collect required data. Changes needed: (1) Connection activities: add session.id tag (from Thrift session handle), add system configuration tags (driver.version, driver.name, runtime.name/version/vendor, os.name/version/arch, client.app_name, locale.name, char_set_encoding, process.name), add connection parameter tags (connection.http_path, connection.host, connection.port, connection.mode, connection.auth_mech, connection.auth_flow, feature.arrow/direct_results/cloudfetch/lz4, connection.batch_size, connection.poll_interval_ms, connection.use_proxy), add auth.type tag. (2) Statement activities: add statement.id tag (operation handle GUID), propagate session.id to child activities, add statement.type (query/update/metadata), add result.format, result.compression_enabled. (3) CloudFetch activities: emit cloudfetch.download_summary event with total_files, successful_downloads, total_time_ms, initial_chunk_latency_ms, slowest_chunk_latency_ms tags. (4) Polling: add poll.count, poll.latency_ms tags.
design_context: Section 4.4.2-4.4.10 of the design doc provides comprehensive field mappings. Section 2.3 explains tag propagation pattern. Sprint plan Task 8. Tags must be set for the MetricsAggregator to extract them.
input_requirements: DatabricksConnection.cs with telemetry integration from task-4.1
Tag definitions in src/Telemetry/TagDefinitions/ (ConnectionOpenEvent, StatementExecutionEvent, ErrorEvent)
Existing Activity infrastructure in the driver
output_deliverables: Modified statement execution files with statement-level tags
Modified CloudFetch download files with chunk metrics tags
exit_criteria: Connection.Open activity has: session.id, auth.type, all driver.* tags, all runtime.* tags, all os.* tags, client.app_name, locale.name, char_set_encoding, process.name, all connection.* tags, all feature.* tags

Existing tests still pass
test_requirements: Tag presence verified in E2E tests (task-5.1)

title: Implement comprehensive E2E tests for the complete telemetry pipeline
description: Create E2E tests that verify the complete telemetry flow from Activity creation through proto export. Create CapturingTelemetryExporter that captures all exported TelemetryFrontendLog instances. Create TelemetryTestHelpers with CreateConnectionWithTelemetry and CreateConnectionWithCapturingTelemetry methods. Tests verify: (1) connection open exports connection event, (2) statement execution exports statement event with execution latency, (3) CloudFetch query exports chunk metrics, (4) invalid SQL exports error event with error.type, (5) feature flag disabled prevents export, (6) multiple connections to same host share telemetry client, (7) circuit breaker stops exporting after threshold failures, (8) graceful shutdown flushes all pending events. All tests use the CapturingTelemetryExporter injected via TestExporterFactory.
design_context: Section 3.2 (Test Injection Pattern) of design doc: CapturingTelemetryExporter captures logs without HTTP calls. TelemetryTestHelpers creates connections with injected exporter. Sprint plan Task 9.
input_requirements: All telemetry components implemented and integrated
Existing E2E test infrastructure at test/E2E/
exit_criteria: CapturingTelemetryExporter implements ITelemetryExporter, captures all exported logs
TelemetryTestHelpers.CreateConnectionWithTelemetry creates connection with injected exporter
TelemetryTestHelpers.CreateConnectionWithCapturingTelemetry returns (connection, capturedLogs)
Tests are marked with [SkippableFact] for environment-dependent tests
Connection event test: open connection -> at least one TelemetryFrontendLog captured
Statement event test: execute SELECT 1 -> log with sql_statement_id and operation_latency_ms
Error event test: invalid SQL -> log with error_info populated
Feature flag disabled test: telemetry.enabled=false -> no logs captured
Multi-connection test: 2 connections same host -> same ITelemetryClient (verified via TelemetryClientManager)
Graceful shutdown test: close with pending events -> all events flushed before close completes
All tests pass when run against live Databricks environment
